My name is Frankie and I am available for adoption. Dont be fooled by my apperence I am A okay! I have something called Dermatis. This is means I have some hairloss, over my face, some parts of my legs and tail. Dermatitis can be related to lots of different things I could have a food allergy, environmental allergy, I could have bacteria on my face that made me have hair loss. I am currently on antibiotics with probiotics between doses, parasite preventative, and topical skin therapy. My caregivers are very good about helping me. Something that also should be noted about me is I have very little eyesight. This never stops me from having a good time or going on long walks! My caregivers notice I still love to play, walk and have yummy treats! Besides my medical history lets talk about what a good boy I am! I was brought in by a good Samaritan on 04/05/2026 as a stray. I am incredibly affectionate and love to give kisses and be around my caregivers! I would be so thrilled to cuddle with you on your couch or bed! I will demand love and attention! I am overall a super adaptable and go with the flow dog. I am outgoing and carefree, and I just can't wait to find my forever home! I weigh 61 lbs and am considered a large breed dog! I walk very well on a leash and only pull sometimes (can you blame me I am just super excited!) My caregivers are also so proud of me that I am so well potty trained! I never go in my kennel and I keep it so clean! I also am incredibly gentle with my stuffies. If you are thinking about adding a sweet and loyal boy to your family I am your guy:) please come and check me out!

My name is Allium and I am available for adoption! I weigh 96 lbs and am considered a "large breed" dog. I am an affectionate lovebug who would be thrilled to cuddle up with you at home! Although German Shepherds are known to be high energy dogs, I am not. I wish to just lay around and be with my people. That doesn't mean that might change in a house so it would be a good idea to know some more about High Energy breeds! We tend to calm down more when the people around us stay calm and enjoy a good play and run around too. We do best when we have lots of exercise and something to keep our mind busy. My caregivers have noticed that I seem to do well with other large dogs. When meeting dogs in a new home, I would do best with a slow introduction. I'm a Playgroup Pal! Ask the staff today about my interactions with other dogs at the shelter in our Playgroups. The staff have noticed that I have the start of seperation anxiety. This isn't uncommon is dogs and can be worked with; with time, patience and training. I just like to be with my people so having a household that works from home or retired would be nice but not a requirement if my family is willing to put the time and effort to help me with my seperation anxiety. You can visit me at the Pet Adoption Center on weekdays from 12:00 pm to 5:00 pm, Saturdays and Sundays from 12:00 pm to 4:00 pm. The Pet Adoption Center is located at 21615 64th Ave S, Kent, WA 98032. Come and visit me today, I'd love to go home with you!

The good news is I`m very food motivated, which makes training fun and rewarding for both of us! I`ve met some dogs at the shelter and seem to do well with pups my size. If you have a dog at home, it`s recommended to bring them by for a meet-and-greet so we can see if we`re a perfect match. I`ve been through a lot, but I haven`t lost my sparkle. I`m ready for a fresh start with a family who will love me, guide me, and let me shine like the star I am! Come meet me...I have a feeling we`re meant to find each other!
